Feature: MHPCO Claim Office (quote and claim operations)
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts
Requirements: The system supports two operations — quote (compute premium for items) and claim (process damage report). There's also a CLI at src/cli.ts that reads JSON from stdin and writes JSON to stdout. See prompt.md for full specification with all rules, examples, integration examples, and edge cases.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, no HITL checkpoints.

content.text: I'll start by reading the specification in `prompt.md` to understand all the rules, examples, and clarifying questions.

content.content:
1	# MHPCO Claim Office Kata
2	
3	## Setting
4	
5	You are building the policy management system for the **Most Honorable
6	Privileged Claims Office for Magical Risks and Cursed Items** (MHPCO),
7	an old-fashioned insurance institution that covers magical items —
8	swords, amulets, staves, potions, and components such as runes and
9	moonstones. The MHPCO is bureaucratic, slightly stingy, and proud of
10	its venerable traditions.
11	
12	## Feature
13	
14	The system supports two operations: **quote** computes the premium for
15	a list of items a customer wishes to insure, and **claim** processes a
16	damage report against an existing policy.
17	
18	## Rules
19	
20	### Item values and base premiums
21	
22	Each main item has an insurance value and a base premium according to
23	the MHPCO price list:
24	
25	- Sword: 1000 G insurance value, 100 G base premium
26	- Amulet: 600 G / 60 G
27	- Staff: 800 G / 80 G
28	- Potion: 400 G / 40 G
29	
30	Components — for example runes and moonstones — are insured at 250 G
31	each, with a base premium of 25 G per component. A building block of
32	3 alike components is offered at a special base premium of 60 G.
33	
34	### Premium modifiers
35	
36	- Cursed items add a 50 % risk surcharge.
37	- Highly enchanted items (enchantment level ≥ 5) add a 30 % risk
38	  surcharge.
39	- Long-standing customers (≥ 2 years of business with MHPCO) receive a
40	  20 % loyalty discount.
41	- A first insurance carries a 10 % initial assessment surcharge.
42	- Customers receive a 15 % discount on each contract after their first.
43	- A 5 G processing fee is added to every premium.
44	
45	All amounts are rounded to whole G in the MHPCO's favor.
46	
47	### Claim processing
48	
49	A deductible of 100 G applies per damage event. The total payout per
50	policy is capped at twice the insurance sum.
51	
52	- Damage to items with enchantment level ≥ 8 is reimbursed at 50 % of
53	  the damage amount.
54	- Damage to items made of dragon material is fully reimbursed.
55	
56	## Examples
57	
58	The examples below illustrate single rules in isolation. Values shown
59	are base premiums or raw payout amounts before integration with other
60	modifiers, unless stated otherwise.
61	
62	### Building block of 3 alike components
63	
64	- 2 runes → 50 G base premium
65	- 3 runes → 60 G base premium (block applies)
66	- 4 runes → 100 G base premium (no block — block requires exactly 3)
67	- 7 runes → 175 G base premium
68	
69	### "Alike" components
70	
71	> ❓ Hmm — "alike" components: does that mean exactly the same type,
72	> or just the same family (rune-y, gemstone-y)?
73	
74	- 2 runes + 1 moonstone → 75 G base premium (no block: different types)
75	- 3 runes + 3 moonstones → 120 G base premium (two separate blocks)
76	
77	### Modifier scope on multi-item policies
78	
79	> ❓ A policy with several items: does the cursed surcharge apply to
80	> the whole policy, or just to the cursed item?
81	
82	- a policy covers a cursed sword (base premium 100 G) and a plain
83	  amulet (base premium 60 G) → policy base premium 160 G; the cursed
84	  surcharge adds 50 G (50 % of the cursed sword's base premium, not
85	  of the policy total) → 210 G before further modifiers and fee
86	- item-specific modifiers (cursed, high enchantment) apply to the
87	  base premium of the affected item; policy-wide modifiers (loyalty,
88	  first insurance, follow-up contract) apply to the policy base
89	  premium (the sum of all item base premiums); the processing fee is
90	  added at the very end
91	
92	### Modifier thresholds
93	
94	- customer with exactly 2 years with MHPCO → loyalty discount applies
95	- sword with exactly enchantment 5 → high-enchantment surcharge
96	  applies; if cursed, both surcharges apply
97	- sword with enchantment 4 → no high-enchantment surcharge; curse
98	  surcharge applies only if cursed
99	- dragon-material sword with exactly enchantment 8, damage 1000 G →
100	  payout 400 G (high-enchantment clause applies, then deductible)
101	
102	### Deductible per damage event
103	
104	- a dragon attack damages an insured sword (500 G) and an insured
105	  amulet (300 G); payout = 600 G (the 100 G deductible applies once
106	  per damaged item)
107	
108	### Standard reimbursement (no special clauses)
109	
110	- regular sword (steel, enchantment 3), damage 500 G → payout 400 G
111	  (full reimbursement minus 100 G deductible; no special clause applies)
112	- damage to a rune (insurance value 250 G), damage 200 G → payout
113	  100 G (full reimbursement minus 100 G deductible; runes have no
114	  enchantment level or material, so no special clause applies)
115	
116	### Enchantment threshold vs. dragon material
117	
118	- dragon-material sword, enchantment 9, damage 1000 G → payout 400 G
119	  (both clauses apply; the 50 % rule wins, then deductible: 500 − 100)
120	- dragon-material sword, enchantment 5, damage 800 G → payout 700 G
121	  (only the dragon-material clause applies: full reimbursement,
122	  then deductible: 800 − 100)
123	- steel sword, enchantment 9, damage 1000 G → payout 400 G
124	  (only the high-enchantment clause applies: 50 % first, then
125	  deductible: 500 − 100)
126	
127	### Multiple items of the same type
128	
129	> ❓ What if a customer brings two of the same item — one in hand,
130	> one in the backpack? Or just two swords because they fight with
131	> two?
132	
133	- a policy covers two swords → insurance sum 2000 G (= 2×1000),
134	  cap 4000 G
135	- a dragon attack damages both swords; `damages` contains two
136	  `{itemType: "sword", ...}` entries → each entry is treated as a
137	  separate damage with its own deductible
138	- if the `damages` array contains more entries of a given type than
139	  the policy actually covers (e.g. two sword damages but only one
140	  sword insured) → the CLI exits with a non-zero status code; the
141	  whole claim is rejected
142	
143	### Cap exhaustion
144	
145	- a policy covers a sword and an amulet → insurance sum 1600 G
146	  (= 1000 + 600, the sum of the items' insurance values), cap 3200 G
147	- a cursed sword (insurance value 1000 G, premium with modifiers
148	  165 G) → cap 2000 G (based on the unmodified insurance value;
149	  premium modifiers do not raise the cap)
150	- a policy covers a sword and 3 runes (a block) → insurance sum
151	  1750 G (= 1000 + 3×250); the block discount affects the premium
152	  only, not the insurance sum
153	- a sword is insured (insurance sum 1000 G, cap 2000 G); two successive
154	  claims of 1500 G each
155	- first claim → payout 1400 G, cap remaining 600 G
156	- second claim → payout 600 G, cap remaining 0 G (the desired 1400 G
157	  is reduced to the remaining cap)
158	
159	### Rounding in the MHPCO's favor
160	
161	- a premium calculation that yields 197.5 G → final premium 198 G
162	  (rounded up)
163	- a payout calculation that yields 350.5 G → final payout 350 G
164	  (rounded down)
165	- intermediate amounts during a calculation are kept as fractions;
166	  only the final premium or payout is rounded
167	
168	### Edge cases
169	
170	- empty item list → premium 5 G (only the processing fee)
171	- quote includes an item with an unknown type (e.g.
172	  `{type: "broomstick"}`) → the CLI exits with a non-zero status code
173	  and writes an error description to stderr; no `results` are written
174	  to stdout
175	- claim references a damage entry whose item is not part of the policy
176	  (e.g. an amulet damaged when only a sword is insured, or an item
177	  with an unknown type) → the CLI exits with a non-zero status code
178	  and writes an error description to stderr
179	- claim contains a damage entry with `amount: -200` → the CLI exits
180	  with a non-zero status code and writes an error description to
181	  stderr
182	
183	## Integration examples
184	
185	The following two examples combine multiple rules to clarify how
186	modifiers stack and how customer history affects the premium.
187	
188	### Newcomer with a cursed sword
189	
190	- customer: 0 years with MHPCO, no previous contract
191	- item: a cursed sword (steel, enchantment 3)
192	- premium: 165 G
193	- (100 G base + 50 G curse + 10 G first insurance = 160 G + 5 G fee = 165 G)
194	
195	### Long-standing customer's second contract
196	
197	> ❓ Wait — does "first insurance" mean the customer's first ever
198	> contract, or the first time we see this particular item? A
199	> long-standing customer with a brand-new sword: does the first
200	> insurance surcharge still apply?
201	
202	- customer: 3 years with MHPCO; this is the customer's second `quote`
203	  in the scenario
204	- item: a cursed sword (steel, enchantment 7)
205	- premium: 160 G
206	- (100 G base + 50 G curse + 30 G high enchantment − 20 G loyalty
207	  + 10 G first insurance − 15 G follow-up contract = 155 G + 5 G fee
208	  = 160 G)
209	- The first insurance surcharge still applies to the new sword, even
210	  though the customer is on a follow-up contract — each item in a
211	  `quote` is treated as a first insurance, regardless of customer
212	  history.
213	
214	## CLI input/output format
215	
216	The implementation must be exposed as a command-line executable
217	`claim-office`. It reads a JSON document from stdin describing a
218	scenario and writes a JSON document with the corresponding results to
219	stdout.
220	
221	A scenario is a JSON object with a `customer` object and a `steps`
222	array. The `customer` describes the single customer for the whole
223	scenario. Each step has an `op` field (`"quote"` or `"claim"`) and the
224	operation-specific input fields. Steps are processed sequentially;
225	later `claim` steps refer to a policy created by an earlier `quote`
226	step via its zero-based step index in a `policy` field.
227	
228	Stdout receives a JSON object with a `results` array of the same length
229	and order as the input `steps`. Each result contains:
230	
231	- For a `quote` step: `premium` (integer, total premium in G).
232	- For a `claim` step: `payout` (integer, payout in G) and
233	  `remainingCap` (integer, cap remaining on the policy after this
234	  claim).
235	
236	### Schema example
237	
238	Stdin:
239	
240	```json
241	{
242	  "customer": {"yearsWithMHPCO": 5},
243	  "steps": [
244	    {
245	      "op": "quote",
246	      "items": [
247	        {"type": "amulet", "material": "silver", "enchantment": 2, "cursed": false}
248	      ]
249	    },
250	    {
251	      "op": "claim",
252	      "policy": 0,
253	      "incident": {
254	        "cause": "fire",
255	        "damages": [
256	          {"itemType": "amulet", "amount": 200}
257	        ]
258	      }
259	    }
260	  ]
261	}
262	```
263	
264	Stdout shape:
265	
266	```json
267	{"results": [{"premium": <integer>}, {"payout": <integer>, "remainingCap": <integer>}]}
268	```
269	
270	## Expected Output Files
271	
272	- Source files implementing the logic and the `claim-office` CLI
273	- Test files
274	- CLI entry point at `src/cli.ts` (reads JSON from stdin, writes JSON to stdout)
275	
276	## Constraints
277	
278	- Use TypeScript for the implementation
279	- The CLI must read JSON from stdin and write JSON to stdout
280	- The CLI entry point must be at `src/cli.ts`
281	- Internal data structures and module organization are free
282
No files found

input.content:
# MHPCO Claim Office -- Example Mapping

## Rules

### R1 -- Item values and base premiums
- Sword: 1000 G insurance value, 100 G base premium
- Amulet: 600 G insurance value, 60 G base premium
- Staff: 800 G insurance value, 80 G base premium
- Potion: 400 G insurance value, 40 G base premium
- Components (rune, moonstone): 250 G insurance value each, 25 G base premium each
- Building block of exactly 3 alike components: 60 G base premium (instead of 75 G)

### R2 -- Premium modifiers (applied in this order)
1. **Item-specific modifiers** (applied to each item's base premium):
   - Cursed: +50% surcharge on the cursed item's base premium
   - Highly enchanted (enchantment >= 5): +30% surcharge on that item's base premium
2. **Policy-wide modifiers** (applied to the sum of all item base premiums):
   - Long-standing customer (yearsWithMHPCO >= 2): -20% loyalty discount
   - First insurance: +10% surcharge (every item in a quote is treated as first insurance, regardless of history)
   - Follow-up contract (customer's 2nd+ quote): -15% discount
3. **Processing fee**: +5 G flat fee added at the very end

### R3 -- Rounding
- All amounts rounded to whole G in MHPCO's favor
- Premium: round UP (ceil)
- Payout: round DOWN (floor)
- Intermediate amounts kept as fractions; only final result rounded

### R4 -- Claim processing
- Deductible: 100 G per damaged item (per entry in the damages array)
- Total payout per policy capped at 2x the insurance sum
- Insurance sum = sum of all items' insurance values (unmodified by premium modifiers)
- Cap tracks across multiple claims against the same policy

### R5 -- Claim reimbursement clauses
- Enchantment >= 8: reimbursed at 50% of damage amount (before deductible)
- Dragon material: fully reimbursed
- Both apply: 50% wins (the enchantment clause takes precedence)
- Neither applies (standard): fully reimbursed
- Components (runes, moonstones): no enchantment level or material, so standard reimbursement

### R6 -- Multiple items of the same type
- Policy can cover multiple items of the same type
- Each damage entry is treated as a separate damage with its own deductible
- If damages array has more entries of a given type than the policy covers, reject with non-zero exit

### R7 -- Edge cases / errors
- Empty item list: premium = 5 G (processing fee only)
- Unknown item type in quote: CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr, no results to stdout
- Claim damage entry for item not in policy: CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr
- Claim damage entry for unknown item type: CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr
- Negative damage amount: CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr

### R8 -- CLI interface
- Entry point: src/cli.ts
- Reads JSON from stdin, writes JSON to stdout
- Input: `{ customer, steps }` where steps are sequential quote/claim operations
- Output: `{ results: [...] }` matching steps array length and order
- Quote result: `{ premium: <integer> }`
- Claim result: `{ payout: <integer>, remainingCap: <integer> }`
- Errors: non-zero exit code, error description to stderr, no results to stdout

---

## Examples

### E1 -- Building block of 3 alike components
- 2 runes: 2 * 25 = 50 G base premium
- 3 runes: 60 G base premium (block discount)
- 4 runes: 4 * 25 = 100 G base premium (no block; block requires exactly 3)
- 7 runes: 7 * 25 = 175 G base premium (no block possible; 7 is not exactly 3)

### E2 -- "Alike" components (type must match exactly)
- 2 runes + 1 moonstone: 75 G (no block; different types)
- 3 runes + 3 moonstones: 120 G (two separate blocks: 60 + 60)

### E3 -- Modifier scope on multi-item policies
- Cursed sword (100 G base) + plain amulet (60 G base) = 160 G policy base
- Curse surcharge: 50% of 100 = 50 G (item-specific, not policy-wide)
- Total before fee: 160 + 50 = 210 G; after fee: 215 G

### E4 -- Modifier thresholds
- Customer with exactly 2 years: loyalty discount applies
- Sword enchantment exactly 5: high-enchantment surcharge applies
- Sword enchantment 4: no high-enchantment surcharge
- If cursed + enchantment 5: both surcharges apply

### E5 -- Deductible per damage event
- Dragon attack damages sword (500 G) and amulet (300 G)
- Payout = (500-100) + (300-100) = 600 G (100 G deductible per item)

### E6 -- Standard reimbursement
- Regular steel sword ench 3, damage 500 G: payout = 500-100 = 400 G
- Rune, damage 200 G: payout = 200-100 = 100 G

### E7 -- Enchantment threshold vs. dragon material
- Dragon sword, ench 9, damage 1000 G: 50% wins, payout = 500-100 = 400 G
- Dragon sword, ench 5, damage 800 G: dragon only, payout = 800-100 = 700 G
- Steel sword, ench 9, damage 1000 G: ench only, payout = 500-100 = 400 G

### E8 -- Dragon-material sword, ench exactly 8, damage 1000 G
- High-enchantment clause applies (ench >= 8): 50% * 1000 = 500, minus 100 deductible = 400 G

### E9 -- Multiple items same type
- Two swords: insurance sum 2000 G, cap 4000 G
- Two sword damages in same event: each gets its own deductible
- More damage entries than insured items of that type: error, non-zero exit

### E10 -- Cap exhaustion
- Sword + amulet: insurance sum = 1600 G, cap = 3200 G
- Cursed sword (1000 G value, premium 165 G): cap = 2000 G (based on unmodified value)
- Sword + 3 runes: insurance sum = 1000 + 750 = 1750 G; block only affects premium
- Cap exhaustion across two claims: sword insured (sum 1000 G, cap 2000 G)
  - Claim 1: damage 1500 G, payout = 1400 G (1500-100), remaining cap = 600 G
  - Claim 2: damage 1500 G, desired payout = 1400 G, actual = 600 G (capped), remaining = 0 G

### E11 -- Rounding
- Premium 197.5 G -> 198 G (ceil)
- Payout 350.5 G -> 350 G (floor)

### E12 -- Integration: newcomer with cursed sword
- Customer: 0 years, no previous contract
- Item: cursed sword (steel, ench 3)
- Base: 100 G + curse 50 G + first-insurance 10 G = 160 G + 5 G fee = 165 G

### E13 -- Integration: long-standing customer's second contract
- Customer: 3 years, this is the 2nd quote
- Item: cursed sword (steel, ench 7)
- Base: 100 G + curse 50 G + high-ench 30 G - loyalty 20 G + first-insurance 10 G - follow-up 15 G = 155 G + 5 G fee = 160 G
- First-insurance surcharge applies to every item regardless of customer history

### E14 -- Empty item list
- Premium = 5 G (processing fee only)

### E15 -- CLI schema
- Input: `{ customer: {yearsWithMHPCO}, steps: [{op, items/policy/incident}] }`
- Output: `{ results: [{premium} | {payout, remainingCap}] }`

---

## Questions / Clarifications

### Q1 -- "Alike" components
Resolved: "alike" means exactly the same component type (e.g., 3 runes, not 2 runes + 1 moonstone). Different component types do not form a block together.

### Q2 -- Modifier scope on multi-item policies
Resolved: Item-specific modifiers (cursed, high enchantment) apply to the individual item's base premium. Policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, first insurance, follow-up) apply to the policy base premium total. Processing fee is last.

### Q3 -- First insurance meaning
Resolved: "First insurance" means every item in every quote is treated as a first insurance. The 10% surcharge always applies to each item. It is NOT about customer history. It applies universally.

### Q4 -- Multiple items of the same type
Resolved: Allowed. Each item is tracked separately in the policy. Damage entries are matched by type count -- if more damages of a type than covered items, the claim is rejected.

---

## Per-test rationale

Each line maps an `it.todo()` to the rule/example it covers.

### Quote tests (simple to complex)

1. "empty item list => premium 5 G (processing fee only)" -- R1, R2.3, E14
2. "single sword => premium 115 G (100 base + 10 first-insurance + 5 fee)" -- R1, R2, basic premium calc
3. "single amulet => premium 77 G (60 base + 6 first-insurance + 5 fee)" -- R1, confirms amulet value
4. "single staff => premium 93 G (80 base + 8 first-insurance + 5 fee)" -- R1, confirms staff value
5. "single potion => premium 49 G (40 base + 4 first-insurance + 5 fee)" -- R1, confirms potion value
6. "single rune => premium 33 G (25 base + 2.5 first-insurance -> ceil(27.5) + 5 = 33)" -- R1, R3 rounding
7. "2 runes => base 50 G (no block)" -- R1, E1
8. "3 runes => base 60 G (block of 3 alike)" -- R1, E1
9. "4 runes => base 100 G (no block; requires exactly 3)" -- R1, E1
10. "7 runes => base 175 G (no block)" -- R1, E1
11. "2 runes + 1 moonstone => base 75 G (no block; different types)" -- R1, E2, Q1
12. "3 runes + 3 moonstones => base 120 G (two separate blocks)" -- R1, E2, Q1
13. "cursed sword => premium 165 G (100 + 50 curse + 10 first-ins + 5 fee)" -- R2, E12 integration
14. "sword with enchantment 5 => high-enchantment surcharge applies" -- R2, E4
15. "sword with enchantment 4 => no high-enchantment surcharge" -- R2, E4
16. "cursed sword with enchantment 5 => both surcharges apply" -- R2, E4
17. "cursed sword + plain amulet => 215 G (curse applies only to sword)" -- R2, E3, Q2
18. "long-standing customer (2 years) => loyalty discount applies" -- R2, E4
19. "customer with 1 year => no loyalty discount" -- R2 (boundary)
20. "second quote => follow-up contract discount applies" -- R2, E13
21. "newcomer cursed sword integration => 165 G" -- E12 full integration
22. "long-standing second contract cursed ench-7 sword => 160 G" -- E13 full integration
23. "premium rounding: 197.5 G rounds to 198 G (ceil)" -- R3, E11

### Claim tests (simple to complex)

24. "standard reimbursement: steel sword ench 3, damage 500 G => payout 400 G" -- R4, R5, E6
25. "rune damage 200 G => payout 100 G (no special clause, deductible applies)" -- R5, E6
26. "dragon-material sword ench 9 damage 1000 G => payout 400 G (50% wins)" -- R5, E7
27. "dragon-material sword ench 5 damage 800 G => payout 700 G (dragon clause only)" -- R5, E7
28. "steel sword ench 9 damage 1000 G => payout 400 G (enchantment clause only)" -- R5, E7
29. "dragon-material sword ench exactly 8 damage 1000 G => payout 400 G" -- R5, E8
30. "deductible per damage event: sword 500 G + amulet 300 G => 600 G total" -- R4, E5
31. "multiple items same type: two swords => insurance sum 2000 G, cap 4000 G" -- R6, E9
32. "two sword damages both get own deductible" -- R6, E9
33. "cap = 2x insurance sum (sword + amulet => cap 3200 G)" -- R4, E10
34. "cursed sword cap based on unmodified insurance value (cap 2000 G)" -- R4, E10

Now let me carefully work through the premium calculation for each test to make sure the expected values are correct, then write the test file.

Let me verify some calculations:

**Single sword (no customer history, first quote):**
- Base: 100 G
- Item modifiers: none
- Policy-wide: first-insurance +10% of 100 = 10 G
- Subtotal: 110 G + 5 G fee = 115 G

**Single amulet:**
- Base: 60 G + first-insurance +10% of 60 = 6 G = 66 G + 5 = 71 G

Wait, let me re-read the spec more carefully. The first-insurance surcharge is a policy-wide modifier applied to the policy base premium. Let me re-examine the integration examples to understand the calculation order.

Integration example 1 (newcomer cursed sword):
- 100 G base
- +50 G curse (item-specific: 50% of 100)
- +10 G first insurance (policy-wide: 10% of base? or 10% of policy base premium?)
- = 160 G + 5 G fee = 165 G

Wait, looking at it: the first-insurance is 10% of the policy base premium (100 G) = 10 G. The curse surcharge (50 G) is item-specific and added separately. So the calculation appears to be:

1. Compute item base premiums: sword = 100 G
2. Add item-specific surcharges: curse = +50% * 100 = 50 G
3. Policy base premium = sum of item base premiums = 100 G
4. Policy-wide modifiers applied to policy base premium: first-insurance = +10% * 100 = 10 G
5. Total before fee: 100 + 50 + 10 = 160 G
6. + 5 G fee = 165 G

Integration example 2 (long-standing, 2nd quote, cursed ench-7 sword):
- 100 G base
- +50 G curse (item: 50% of 100)
- +30 G high-ench (item: 30% of 100)
- -20 G loyalty (policy: -20% of 100)
- +10 G first-insurance (policy: +10% of 100)
- -15 G follow-up (policy: -15% of 100)
- = 100 + 50 + 30 - 20 + 10 - 15 = 155 G + 5 G fee = 160 G

So the pattern is:
- Policy base premium = sum of all item base premiums (without item-specific modifiers)
- Item-specific surcharges are computed as percentage of the individual item's base premium and added
- Policy-wide modifiers are computed as percentage of the policy base premium
- Fee added last

Now for **single amulet (0 years, first quote)**:
- Policy base = 60 G
- Item modifiers: none
- Policy-wide: first-insurance = +10% * 60 = 6 G
- Subtotal: 60 + 6 = 66 G + 5 G fee = 71 G

**Single staff**: 80 + 8 = 88 + 5 = 93 G
**Single potion**: 40 + 4 = 44 + 5 = 49 G
**Single rune**: 25 + 2.5 = 27.5 -> ceil = 28 + 5 = 33 G

1	# MHPCO Claim Office -- Example Mapping
2	
3	## Rules
4	
5	### R1 -- Item values and base premiums
6	- Sword: 1000 G insurance value, 100 G base premium
7	- Amulet: 600 G insurance value, 60 G base premium
8	- Staff: 800 G insurance value, 80 G base premium
9	- Potion: 400 G insurance value, 40 G base premium
10	- Components (rune, moonstone): 250 G insurance value each, 25 G base premium each
11	- Building block of exactly 3 alike components: 60 G base premium (instead of 75 G)
12	
13	### R2 -- Premium modifiers (applied in this order)
14	1. **Item-specific modifiers** (applied to each item's base premium):
15	   - Cursed: +50% surcharge on the cursed item's base premium
16	   - Highly enchanted (enchantment >= 5): +30% surcharge on that item's base premium
17	2. **Policy-wide modifiers** (applied to the sum of all item base premiums):
18	   - Long-standing customer (yearsWithMHPCO >= 2): -20% loyalty discount
19	   - First insurance: +10% surcharge (every item in a quote is treated as first insurance, regardless of history)
20	   - Follow-up contract (customer's 2nd+ quote): -15% discount
21	3. **Processing fee**: +5 G flat fee added at the very end
22	
23	### R3 -- Rounding
24	- All amounts rounded to whole G in MHPCO's favor
25	- Premium: round UP (ceil)
26	- Payout: round DOWN (floor)
27	- Intermediate amounts kept as fractions; only final result rounded
28	
29	### R4 -- Claim processing
30	- Deductible: 100 G per damaged item (per entry in the damages array)
31	- Total payout per policy capped at 2x the insurance sum
32	- Insurance sum = sum of all items' insurance values (unmodified by premium modifiers)
33	- Cap tracks across multiple claims against the same policy
34	
35	### R5 -- Claim reimbursement clauses
36	- Enchantment >= 8: reimbursed at 50% of damage amount (before deductible)
37	- Dragon material: fully reimbursed
38	- Both apply: 50% wins (the enchantment clause takes precedence)
39	- Neither applies (standard): fully reimbursed
40	- Components (runes, moonstones): no enchantment level or material, so standard reimbursement
41	
42	### R6 -- Multiple items of the same type
43	- Policy can cover multiple items of the same type
44	- Each damage entry is treated as a separate damage with its own deductible
45	- If damages array has more entries of a given type than the policy covers, reject with non-zero exit
46	
47	### R7 -- Edge cases / errors
48	- Empty item list: premium = 5 G (processing fee only)
49	- Unknown item type in quote: CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr, no results to stdout
50	- Claim damage entry for item not in policy: CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr
51	- Claim damage entry for unknown item type: CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr
52	- Negative damage amount: CLI exits non-zero, error to stderr
53	
54	### R8 -- CLI interface
55	- Entry point: src/cli.ts
56	- Reads JSON from stdin, writes JSON to stdout
57	- Input: `{ customer, steps }` where steps are sequential quote/claim operations
58	- Output: `{ results: [...] }` matching steps array length and order
59	- Quote result: `{ premium: <integer> }`
60	- Claim result: `{ payout: <integer>, remainingCap: <integer> }`
61	- Errors: non-zero exit code, error description to stderr, no results to stdout
62	
63	---
64	
65	## Examples
66	
67	### E1 -- Building block of 3 alike components
68	- 2 runes: 2 * 25 = 50 G base premium
69	- 3 runes: 60 G base premium (block discount)
70	- 4 runes: 4 * 25 = 100 G base premium (no block; block requires exactly 3)
71	- 7 runes: 7 * 25 = 175 G base premium (no block possible; 7 is not exactly 3)
72	
73	### E2 -- "Alike" components (type must match exactly)
74	- 2 runes + 1 moonstone: 75 G (no block; different types)
75	- 3 runes + 3 moonstones: 120 G (two separate blocks: 60 + 60)
76	
77	### E3 -- Modifier scope on multi-item policies
78	- Cursed sword (100 G base) + plain amulet (60 G base) = 160 G policy base
79	- Curse surcharge: 50% of 100 = 50 G (item-specific, not policy-wide)
80	- Total before fee: 160 + 50 = 210 G; after fee: 215 G
81	
82	### E4 -- Modifier thresholds
83	- Customer with exactly 2 years: loyalty discount applies
84	- Sword enchantment exactly 5: high-enchantment surcharge applies
85	- Sword enchantment 4: no high-enchantment surcharge
86	- If cursed + enchantment 5: both surcharges apply
87	
88	### E5 -- Deductible per damage event
89	- Dragon attack damages sword (500 G) and amulet (300 G)
90	- Payout = (500-100) + (300-100) = 600 G (100 G deductible per item)
91	
92	### E6 -- Standard reimbursement
93	- Regular steel sword ench 3, damage 500 G: payout = 500-100 = 400 G
94	- Rune, damage 200 G: payout = 200-100 = 100 G
95	
96	### E7 -- Enchantment threshold vs. dragon material
97	- Dragon sword, ench 9, damage 1000 G: 50% wins, payout = 500-100 = 400 G
98	- Dragon sword, ench 5, damage 800 G: dragon only, payout = 800-100 = 700 G
99	- Steel sword, ench 9, damage 1000 G: ench only, payout = 500-100 = 400 G
100	
101	### E8 -- Dragon-material sword, ench exactly 8, damage 1000 G
102	- High-enchantment clause applies (ench >= 8): 50% * 1000 = 500, minus 100 deductible = 400 G
103	
104	### E9 -- Multiple items same type
105	- Two swords: insurance sum 2000 G, cap 4000 G
106	- Two sword damages in same event: each gets its own deductible
107	- More damage entries than insured items of that type: error, non-zero exit
108	
109	### E10 -- Cap exhaustion
110	- Sword + amulet: insurance sum = 1600 G, cap = 3200 G
111	- Cursed sword (1000 G value, premium 165 G): cap = 2000 G (based on unmodified value)
112	- Sword + 3 runes: insurance sum = 1000 + 750 = 1750 G; block only affects premium
113	- Cap exhaustion across two claims: sword insured (sum 1000 G, cap 2000 G)
114	  - Claim 1: damage 1500 G, payout = 1400 G (1500-100), remaining cap = 600 G
115	  - Claim 2: damage 1500 G, desired payout = 1400 G, actual = 600 G (capped), remaining = 0 G
116	
117	### E11 -- Rounding
118	- Premium 197.5 G -> 198 G (ceil)
119	- Payout 350.5 G -> 350 G (floor)
120	
121	### E12 -- Integration: newcomer with cursed sword
122	- Customer: 0 years, no previous contract
123	- Item: cursed sword (steel, ench 3)
124	- Base: 100 G + curse 50 G + first-insurance 10 G = 160 G + 5 G fee = 165 G
125	
126	### E13 -- Integration: long-standing customer's second contract
127	- Customer: 3 years, this is the 2nd quote
128	- Item: cursed sword (steel, ench 7)
129	- Base: 100 G + curse 50 G + high-ench 30 G - loyalty 20 G + first-insurance 10 G - follow-up 15 G = 155 G + 5 G fee = 160 G
130	- First-insurance surcharge applies to every item regardless of customer history
131	
132	### E14 -- Empty item list
133	- Premium = 5 G (processing fee only)
134	
135	### E15 -- CLI schema
136	- Input: `{ customer: {yearsWithMHPCO}, steps: [{op, items/policy/incident}] }`
137	- Output: `{ results: [{premium} | {payout, remainingCap}] }`
138	
139	---
140	
141	## Questions / Clarifications
142	
143	### Q1 -- "Alike" components
144	Resolved: "alike" means exactly the same component type (e.g., 3 runes, not 2 runes + 1 moonstone). Different component types do not form a block together.
145	
146	### Q2 -- Modifier scope on multi-item policies
147	Resolved: Item-specific modifiers (cursed, high enchantment) apply to the individual item's base premium. Policy-wide modifiers (loyalty, first insurance, follow-up) apply to the policy base premium total. Processing fee is last.
148	
149	### Q3 -- First insurance meaning
150	Resolved: "First insurance" means every item in every quote is treated as a first insurance. The 10% surcharge always applies to each item. It is NOT about customer history. It applies universally.
151	
152	### Q4 -- Multiple items of the same type
153	Resolved: Allowed. Each item is tracked separately in the policy. Damage entries are matched by type count -- if more damages of a type than covered items, the claim is rejected.
